# Tasks

Working task list for the keypad designer. Pulled from ROADMAP.md and prioritized for the next stretch of work. Check items off as they ship.

## P0 — `.dkf` exporter (the main unblock)

The sample file `ESP_Survey_ACAD.dkf` confirms the format is **plain ASCII with CRLF line endings**, not binary. Writing a real exporter is now feasible. Update README + ROADMAP language once this lands — they currently claim the format is undocumented.

### Format spec (derived from ESP_Survey_ACAD.dkf)

```
*Generated by the DAT/EM Keypad
Filename: <abs-path>
*Date: <Day, Month DD YYYY>
*Time: HH:MM
PORTRAIT: YES|NO
ACTIVE_LAYER: <name>

LAYER: <name>
BUTTON:<n>
\tBUTTON SHAPE: <w> <h>
\tBACKGROUND COLOR: R;G;B
\tHEADER: TRUE                          # optional, header-style cell
\tSTART BUTTON: <n>                     # slave cells point at master, no other fields
\tDATA: <macro with {RET} for Enter>
\tJUMP LAYER: <layer-name>              # optional
\tLABEL:                                # repeatable for multi-line labels
\t\tLABEL TEXT: <text>
\t\tWEIGHT: <int>                      # optional, e.g. 700 = bold
\t\tHEIGHT: <int>                      # optional, negative shrinks
\t\tPITCH & FAMILY: <int>              # optional
\t\tFONTNAME: <name>                   # optional
\t\tFOREGROUND COLOR: R;G;B            # optional
\t\tIS TEXT: TRUE|FALSE                # optional, FALSE means bitmap
\t\tBITMAP NAME: <file.bmp>            # when IS TEXT:FALSE
```

Confirmed: a "full" keypad is **252 buttons per layer** (sample has 2 layers × 252 buttons = 504 total).

### Tasks

- [x] Add `js/dkf.js` with `buildDkfExport(project)` that writes a valid `.dkf` string for the current project.
- [x] **Macro syntax translation.** Replace `;` and `\n` with `{RET}`, ensure trailing `{RET}`. Implemented as `macroToDkf(commands)`. Caveat: doesn't yet skip `;` inside quoted strings — none of the prompt-emitted macros use those, but flag if it ever comes up.
- [x] **Color mapping.** Hex → `R;G;B` decimal via `hexToRgb` in `dkf.js`.
- [x] **Multi-cell buttons.** Schema extended with optional `width` / `height` on master buttons. Renderer uses CSS grid spans, slave cells are skipped (master claims them). Editor exposes W/H number inputs with collision + off-grid validation. Exporter emits `BUTTON SHAPE: W H` for masters and `START BUTTON: <n>` for slaves. Importer preserves spans (no longer flattens). `clampSpansToGrid` runs on grid resize. Verified: 16 masters round-trip, two consecutive re-exports byte-identical.
- [x] **Grid → button numbering.** Confirmed row-major from top-left, 14 cols × 18 rows (verified against sample's row-1 headers).
- [x] **Layers.** v1 emits a single `LAYER: Main`. Multi-layer + `JUMP LAYER` deferred.
- [x] Hook export into Export menu — added `DAT/EM .dkf` button alongside Reference text / JSON backup. Clamp warning (rows>18 or cols>14) shown via toast on mode switch.
- [ ] **Manual browser verification.** Open `index.html`, click Export → DAT/EM .dkf, confirm textarea shows the macro and Download produces a `.dkf` that DAT/EM Keypad Editor can open. (Blocked: no Keypad Editor available locally — needs a Windows machine.)
- [x] **Round-trip test.** Parse sample → re-emit → re-parse cleanly. Sample's 187 content-bearing buttons in `Main` layer round-trip with zero further loss after the first lossy import. `/tmp/roundtrip.dkf` has identical top-level structure to the original.
- [x] **`.dkf` importer.** `js/dkf-import.js`: `parseDkf(text)` returns `{project, dropped}`. Wired into the existing Import modal with format auto-detect (JSON vs `.dkf`). First-import losses on the sample: 1 extra layer skipped, 7 bitmaps dropped. Spans, headers, and stacked labels now all round-trip losslessly.
- [x] **Stacked-label rendering.** Single-line labels containing " / " or newlines were causing Keypad Controller to truncate cell text. Exporter now splits on those separators and emits one `LABEL:` block per segment, mirroring how the importer joins them. Verified: sample's BUTTON:15 EOP/Pvmt now emits 2 stacked LABEL blocks matching the original byte-for-byte.
- [x] **Color mapping fidelity.** Added 3 workflow-class categories — `roads` (yellow), `utility` (red), `dtm` (magenta) — to fill perceptual gaps in the palette. Sample's color distribution now splits sensibly: roads 41, utility 24, dtm 11, mixed 28 (was 69 → mixed before). AI prompts in `prompts.js` updated to know about the new categories. Round-trip color distribution is stable.
- [ ] Update README "Compatibility notes" — drop the line about manual transcription being required (do this once browser verification passes).
- [ ] Update ROADMAP — move `.dkf` exporter out of "Near-term" once shipped.

## P1 — Correctness fixes the sample exposed

- [ ] **Audit prompts.js for `;` vs `{RET}`.** If we keep generating `;`-style macros and translating at export time, document that. If we switch the canonical form to `{RET}`, retrain the prompts.
- [x] **Header-style buttons.** Added optional `header: true` flag to button schema. Editor has a "Section header" checkbox; renderer styles header cells with thicker border + uppercase + bolder label; `dkf.js` emits `HEADER: TRUE`; `dkf-import.js` preserves the flag (was previously dropped). Verified end-to-end on sample: 4 in → 4 out → 4 re-imported.
- [x] **Bitmap labels (icons on keys).** `BITMAP NAME` in the dkf format now round-trips: importer captures it into `btn.bitmap`, exporter emits it as a `LABEL: / IS TEXT:FALSE / BITMAP NAME:<file>` block. Editor side panel has an "Icon (BMP filename)" text input with a `<datalist>` of the 7 known names from the sample (cross.bmp, hcntl.bmp, vcntl.bmp, hvcntl.bmp, inddep.bmp, paspnt.bmp, photoc.bmp); users can also type any other filename DAT/EM has on disk. Renderer shows a small monospace tag with an image icon when a cell uses a bitmap. Verified: all 7 bitmaps from the sample preserved through full import → export → re-import.
- [x] **Custom bitmap upload + ZIP export.** New "Upload custom icon…" button in the editor accepts any image format the browser can decode (PNG, JPG, BMP, GIF, WebP). `js/bmp.js` letterboxes onto a 24×24 white canvas, encodes as a 24-bit Windows BMP byte-for-byte (BITMAPFILEHEADER + BITMAPINFOHEADER + bottom-up BGR pixel rows with 4-byte row padding) — no third-party deps. Stored as a base64 dataURL on `project.customBitmaps[filename]`. The editor shows a 36px thumbnail preview; cell renders the actual image (pixelated rendering preserved). A new fourth export mode "ZIP (.dkf + bitmaps)" bundles the `.dkf` + every referenced custom BMP using a hand-written store-mode ZIP encoder (`js/zip.js`, with proper CRC32 and central directory records — `unzip -l` happy with output). The export modal preview lists exactly what's in the bundle and which bitmaps reference filenames that aren't bundled (i.e. must already exist in the user's DAT/EM bitmap folder).

- [x] **Bulk grid operations.** Cmd/Ctrl-click toggles a cell into a multi-select; Shift-click extends a rectangular range from the anchor cell. Selected cells get a blue outline. A floating action bar at the bottom shows the count and exposes: recolor by clicking a swatch, find/replace across labels/macros/notes, bulk delete, clear. ESC clears the selection. All ops persist + re-render automatically.
- [x] **Drag-to-rearrange buttons.** HTML5 drag-and-drop on configured cells. Source highlights faded, target gets a green dashed outline. Multi-cell spans move as a unit and validate their full footprint against the target — off-grid or collisions reject with a toast. Was the top remaining ROADMAP near-term item.
- [x] **Undo / redo.** Per-project history stack (capped 50, with redo). `recordChange()` snapshots the current project before each mutator (button save/clear, bulk recolor/delete/find-replace, drag-move, DXF generate, AI autofill, grid resize, clear-all, context edit, rename). `Cmd/Ctrl+Z` undoes, `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z` (or `Cmd/Ctrl+Y`) redoes. Skips when focus is in a text field so browser-native text undo still works. Stack resets on project switch/create/duplicate/delete/import.
- [x] **Template library.** Optional `isTemplate` flag per project. Toolbar gets a ☆/★ toggle (current project) and a "📋 From…" picker. The picker lists every project — templates first with a yellow tint — and "Use this" clones the chosen one into a new working project (name prompt; the clone drops the `isTemplate` flag). Project dropdown prefixes template projects with ★ and sorts them above non-templates. Useful workflow: finish a project's keypad once for a job, ★ it, then start every future job from that template + DXF + find-replace layer prefixes.
- [x] **`.dwg` / `.dwt` direct support.** Drop a binary AutoCAD drawing/template and the importer extracts the LAYER table directly — no DXF export step. Powered by `@mlightcad/libredwg-web` (WASM port of LibreDWG) shipped as a vendored asset at `vendor/libredwg/`. The 6.3 MB WASM + 8.8 MB JS wrapper are lazy-loaded on first binary-file drop only — base page weight is unchanged. Verified against the user's AC1032 (AutoCAD 2018) DWT: 381 layers extracted with names, ACI colors, and on/off/frozen flags.
- [x] **Entity-aware layer filter.** Both DXF and DWG parsers now also walk the ENTITIES section / `db.entities` and tally per-layer entity counts. UI shows the count in each layer row (green "N entities" / dim italic "no entities"), groups now sort most-active layer first, and a "Hide layers with no entities" checkbox is on by default. Pre-selection prefers V- layers with content over all V- layers when the entity distribution is meaningful (auto-disabled for templates where every layer is empty or has stub entities). On the user's real DXF: pre-select dropped from 80 noisy V- layers to **36 V- layers that actually have collected geometry**.
- [x] **AI-generated layer report (CSV).** New "📊 Layer report" button in the CAD import modal asks Claude to describe every mapped layer (entityCount > 0), then downloads a CSV with name, entity count, ACI color, on/off state, frozen flag, and AI description. Uses `describeLayers(names)` in `ai.js` — single batch call leveraging AIA/NCS naming conventions to infer feature class from the layer name. CSV opens transparently in Excel/Numbers/Sheets — no SheetJS dependency added.

## P2 — UX polish from the README review

- [ ] Add the missing `docs/screenshot.png` referenced from README.md, or remove the reference.
- [ ] Replace the placeholder `github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/datem-keypad-designer.git` clone URL once the repo has a real home.
- [ ] Surface keyboard shortcuts (`Cmd/Ctrl+Enter` save, `Esc` close) somewhere in the UI — a "?" help popover is fine.
- [ ] Drag-to-rearrange buttons within the grid (from ROADMAP "Near-term").
- [ ] Tighten the side-panel layout on narrow viewports (tablet usability).

## P3 — Nice-to-haves

- [x] **Symbology library import (DXF flavor).** Drop a CAD `.dxf` template into the new "📐 From CAD" modal; parser extracts the LAYER table (name, ACI color, on/off, frozen), groups by AIA/NCS discipline prefix, pre-selects V- layers. Generates one button per selected layer with macros that auto-categorize colors by name pattern (V-ROAD-* → roads, V-UTIL-* → utility, V-TOPO-BRKL → dtm). Tested against a real 211-layer template: 80 V- layers, mapped cleanly. CSV/XML variants and `.dwg`/`.dwt` direct support still open.
- [x] **Drawing-tool inference per generated button.** Each layer button now chains the right drawing tool after the layer switch: breaklines → `_AECCDRAWFEATURELINES`, spots → `_AECCCREATEPTMANUAL`, poles/manholes/valves/hydrants/signs/trees → `_POINT`, annotation/text → none, everything else linear → `_3DPOLY`. All vanilla AutoCAD/Civil 3D — no DAT/EM Capture required. Toggleable in the import modal.
- [x] **Auto section headers for DXF-generated layouts.** Selected layers are grouped by category, each section starts on a fresh row with a 2-wide colored `HEADER: TRUE` cell labeled `ROADS` / `UTILITIES` / `DTM` / `LAYERS`. Cosmetic but makes generated layouts look like real production keypads. Toggleable.
- [x] **Macro lint.** New `js/lint.js` runs static analysis on every button's macro. Rules: empty-macro advisory, missing trailing `;;` on `-LAYER` (warn), bare `LAYER` instead of `-LAYER` (warn), missing `^C^C` cancel prefix (info), case-wrong Summit keywords like `driver` vs `Driver` (warn), and use of DAT/EM Capture commands that require Capture loaded (info — explains the autofill behavior we hit). Editor side panel shows per-button warnings live as you type; status bar surfaces a project-wide count and tints red when any `warn`-level issue exists. Conservative ruleset — false positives kept low so users don't tune the warnings out.
- [ ] Hosted version with backend proxy (eliminates BYOK).

## Reference

- Sample `.dkf` lives one directory up from this repo: `../ESP_Survey_ACAD.dkf` (252 buttons × 2 layers, includes `START BUTTON`, `JUMP LAYER`, `BITMAP NAME`, `HEADER` examples).
- Companion CAD template: `../ESP_Survey Template.dwt` (AutoCAD 2018-format DWT). Useful for cross-referencing layer names that appear in the sample's `DATA` lines (e.g. `V-ROAD-PAVE`, `V-BLDG-DECK`).
